context:
<Profile>### IDENTITY
- **Name:** Mia Thompson  
- **Age:** 30  
- **Gender:** Female  
- **Experience level:** **Intermediate** — 2 years of serious, consistent training; alpine ski racer training year-round (off-season strength/power emphasis, in-season maintenance).

---

### GOALS
- **Primary goal(s):** **Performance (alpine ski racing, amateur/masters)** — off-season emphasis on:
  - Leg power & explosive output (jump/plyo capacity, rate of force development)
  - Single-leg strength (force production + deceleration control)
  - Knee/hip stability & injury prevention (especially right knee)
  - Core strength (anti-rotation/anti-extension; trunk stiffness under load)
- **Secondary goals:**
  - Maintain athleticism and robustness into in-season (reduce injury risk; tolerate volume)
- **Target events/deadlines:**
  - **Ski season in ~8 months (as of 2026-02-27)** — off-season build phase leading into pre-season specificity.

---

## TRAINING CONTEXT

### Schedule & Availability
- **Available training days:** **Mon / Tue / Thu / Sat**
- **Desired training frequency:** **4 sessions/week**
- **Session duration:** **60–75 min**
- **Time windows:** **Mornings** (all training days)

### Equipment & Environment
- **Training location:** **Hybrid**
  - **Commercial gym:** barbells, dumbbells, machines, plyo boxes (full access)
  - **Home (quick sessions):** some equipment available (details/loads not specified yet)

### Constraints
- **[ACTIVE]** Previous **right knee MCL sprain** — currently managed with prehab/stability work; monitor knee valgus/rotation stress and cutting/plyo volume tolerance (**noted 2026-02-27**).

### Preferences
- Prefers **explosive movements, plyometrics, heavy single-leg work**
- Training style emphasis: power + stability + injury-prep (ski-specific robustness)

---

## METRICS
*(No quantitative performance metrics provided yet — baseline testing needed.)*

### Strength (ski supplemental / primary off-season driver)
- **Not yet recorded (as of 2026-02-27):** key lifts and single-leg strength benchmarks (e.g., back/front squat, trap bar deadlift, split squat/Bulgarian, RDL, step-ups; rep maxes or estimated 1RMs)

### Endurance (supportive)
- **Not yet recorded (as of 2026-02-27):** weekly conditioning volume, modality, HR zones, or interval benchmarks

### Movement Quality (priority for injury prevention)
- **Not yet recorded (as of 2026-02-27):** knee stability/tolerance markers (single-leg squat quality, hop/landing mechanics, pain ratings, ROM symmetry)

### Body Composition (optional/secondary unless specified)
- **Not provided (as of 2026-02-27)**

---

## LOG
## 2026-02-27 — Profile created/updated (intake)
- Confirmed athlete context: **amateur/masters alpine ski racer**, off-season performance focus.
- Availability set to **4x/week (Mon/Tue/Thu/Sat mornings), 60–75 min**.
- Equipment confirmed: **commercial gym + some home equipment** (home inventory pending).
- Constraint added: **[ACTIVE] prior right MCL sprain** managed with prehab/stability.
- Metrics section left blank pending baseline testing (strength, movement quality, conditioning).

**Field consistency check (as of 2026-02-27):**
- Schedule supports desired frequency (4 days listed = 4 sessions/week).
- Experience label aligns with history (2 years serious training = Intermediate).
- Deadline captured (8 months until season; anchored to current date).</Profile>

reference.response:
## Header
- **Program name:** Alpine Off-Season Power + Single-Leg Strength (Knee-Stable) — 4 Days
- **Program Owner:** GymText
- **User:** Mia Thompson
- **Goal:** Alpine ski racing performance — leg power/RFD, single-leg strength + decel control, knee/hip stability (R knee), trunk stiffness
- **Duration:** **Ongoing with phase cycling** (8-month runway to ski season)

---

## Program Philosophy
Mia is an intermediate, year-round alpine ski racer who needs high-output legs (power + eccentric control), durable single-leg strength, and knee/hip stability without aggravating a prior right MCL sprain. A **4-day split (Mon/Tue/Thu/Sat)** fits her schedule while letting us place **high-neural power work early in sessions** and space harder lower-body exposures for recovery and tissue tolerance. The program progresses from **tissue capacity + unilateral foundations → max strength → power/RFD → ski-specific repeated power & deceleration**, while keeping **knee valgus/rotation stress managed** via controlled progressions, landing mechanics, and targeted adductor/hip work. Progression is primarily **RPE-based with planned deloads** (every 4th week) so output stays high and knee symptoms stay quiet.

---

# Phase 1: Foundation & Tissue Capacity (Weeks 1–6)
Build unilateral strength base, improve landing mechanics, increase posterior chain and trunk capacity, and accumulate knee-friendly volume.

## Weekly Pattern

**Mon — Lower Strength + Landing Mechanics**
- **Focus:** Knee-dominant strength + controlled decel/landing
- **Volume:** ~16–22 working sets
- Box Jump (step-down): 4 × 2 @ crisp/fast (full recovery) — *power without high landing volume*
- Front Squat or Safety Bar Squat: 4 × 5 @ RPE 7
- Rear-Foot-Elevated Split Squat: 3 × 6–8/side @ RPE 7–8 — *single-leg force + knee tracking*
- Hamstring Curl (machine): 3 × 10–12 @ RPE 8 — *hamstring support for knee stability*
- Copenhagen Plank (short lever → long lever as tolerated): 2–3 × 15–25s/side — *adductor capacity (MCL-friendly support)*
- Anti-rotation Press (Pallof): 3 × 10–12/side @ controlled tempo

**Tue — Upper Strength + Trunk Stiffness**
- **Focus:** Upper strength + anti-extension/anti-rotation core
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets
- Bench Press (or DB bench): 4 × 4–6 @ RPE 7–8
- Weighted Pull-Up / Lat Pulldown: 4 × 5–8 @ RPE 7–8
- Half-Kneeling Landmine Press: 3 × 8/side @ RPE 7 — *trunk + shoulder stability*
- Chest-Supported Row: 3 × 8–10 @ RPE 8
- Dead Bug (loaded if strong): 3 × 6–10/side — *anti-extension*
- Side Plank w/ Row (cable/band): 2 × 8–12/side — *lateral trunk stiffness*

**Thu — Lower Hinge + Lateral/Frontal Plane Prep**
- **Focus:** Posterior chain strength + lateral control (knee-safe)
- **Volume:** ~16–22 working sets
- Trap Bar Deadlift: 4 × 4–6 @ RPE 7
- Lateral Bound to Stick (small amplitude): 4 × 3/side @ smooth stick — *introduces ski-relevant lateral force with controlled landing*
- Single-Leg RDL (DB): 3 × 8/side @ RPE 7 — *hip control + hamstrings*
- Step-Down (6–10"): 3 × 6–8/side @ slow 3-sec eccentric — *deceleration + knee tracking*
- Calf Raise (straight knee): 3 × 10–15 @ RPE 8 — *ankle stiffness for landing*
- Hip Airplane (assisted): 2 × 4–6/side — *hip external rotator control (valgus management)*

**Sat — Athletic Hypertrophy + Conditioning (Low Knee Irritation)**
- **Focus:** Robustness, work capacity, trunk; keep joints fresh
- **Volume:** ~14–20 working sets
- Med Ball Scoop Toss (rotational) OR Chest Pass: 5 × 3 @ explosive — *power through trunk/hips*
- Leg Press (feet position to keep knee comfortable): 3 × 10 @ RPE 8
- Seated Row or Cable Row: 3 × 10–12 @ RPE 8
- DB Romanian Deadlift: 3 × 10 @ RPE 7–8
- Farmer Carry: 4 × 20–30m heavy — *trunk stiffness + grip*
- Bike/Erg Intervals (knee-friendly): 8–12 min total (e.g., 6 × 30s hard / 60s easy)

## Progression Strategy (Phase 1)
- **Weeks 1–3:** Add reps first (within range), then load while keeping **RPE 7–8** on main lifts.
- **Week 4 (Deload):** Cut volume ~40–50% (e.g., 2–3 work sets instead of 4), keep intensity moderate (RPE ~6–7), keep plyos but halve contacts.
- **Weeks 5–6:** Slight load increase on main lifts (2.5–5%), progress single-leg variations by load or ROM; lateral bounds increase amplitude only if landings are quiet and knee feels stable.
- **Advance to Phase 2 when:** knee tolerates current plyo contacts without next-day flare, and main lift technique is consistent at RPE 8.

---

# Phase 2: Max Strength Emphasis (Weeks 7–14)
Drive higher force production (key for power later) while maintaining knee-friendly unilateral work and controlled plyos.

## Weekly Pattern

**Mon — Lower Max Strength (Squat Pattern) + Low-Contact Power**
- **Focus:** Heavy squat pattern + potentiation jumps
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets
- Countermovement Jump (no tuck, stick landing): 5 × 2 (full recovery)
- Front Squat / SSB Squat: 5 × 3–4 @ RPE 8
- RFESS (slightly heavier): 3 × 5–6/side @ RPE 8
- Nordic Curl (assisted) OR Ham Curl: 3 × 6–10 @ RPE 8 — *hamstrings for knee resilience*
- Copenhagen Plank: 3 × 20–30s/side
- Pallof Press: 2–3 × 8–10/side (heavier)

**Tue — Upper Max Strength**
- **Focus:** Press/pull strength + trunk
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets
- Bench Press: 5 × 3–5 @ RPE 8
- Weighted Pull-Up / Heavy Pulldown: 5 × 3–6 @ RPE 8
- DB Incline Press: 3 × 8 @ RPE 7–8
- One-Arm Cable Row: 3 × 8/side @ RPE 8
- Ab Wheel (or long-lever plank): 3 × 6–10 — *anti-extension under load*

**Thu — Lower Max Strength (Hinge Pattern) + Lateral Control**
- **Focus:** Heavy hinge + decel/lateral integrity
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets
- Trap Bar Deadlift: 5 × 3 @ RPE 8
- Lateral Bound to Stick: 5 × 2–3/side (quality > height/distance)
- Single-Leg RDL: 3 × 6–8/side @ RPE 8
- Step-Down (progress height/load): 3 × 5–6/side @ controlled eccentric
- Calf Raise: 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 8

**Sat — Power Endurance / Hypertrophy Support**
- **Focus:** Maintain muscle + build repeatability without trashing legs
- **Volume:** ~12–18 working sets
- Med Ball Throw (rotational or overhead): 6 × 2–3
- Split Squat (front foot elevated optional): 3 × 8/side @ RPE 7
- Row + Press superset (machine/cables): 3 × 10–12 each @ RPE 7–8
- Farmer Carry or Suitcase Carry: 4 × 20–40m — *anti-lateral flexion*
- Conditioning (bike/erg): 10–15 min Zone 2–3 OR 8 × 20s brisk / 70s easy

## Progression Strategy (Phase 2)
- Main lifts use **double progression**:
  - Hit top of rep range at target RPE → add load next week (2.5–5%).
- Keep plyos **low contact but high intent**; only add contacts if landings remain silent and stable.
- **Deload Week 4 of the phase (Week 10 overall):** reduce working sets by ~50%, keep intensity RPE 6–7.
- Transition when: squat/hinge patterns are moving heavier at RPE 8 with no knee irritation trend, and unilateral strength is improving without valgus collapse.

---

# Phase 3: Power, RFD & Reactive Strength (Weeks 15–22)
Convert strength into ski-relevant power: faster intent, more reactive plyos, and contrast work while managing knee stress.

## Weekly Pattern

**Mon — Contrast Lower (Force → Speed)**
- **Focus:** High output; contrast pairs
- **Volume:** ~12–16 working sets + plyo contacts
- A1) Front Squat / SSB Squat: 4 × 2–3 @ RPE 8  
  A2) Box Jump OR Jump Squat (light): 4 × 2–3 — *contrast to improve RFD*
- RFESS (moderate, fast concentric): 3 × 5/side @ RPE 7–8
- Ham Curl: 3 × 8 @ RPE 8
- Pallof Press: 3 × 8/side (heavier, crisp)

**Tue — Upper Power + Strength Maintenance**
- **Focus:** Explosive upper + maintain base strength
- **Volume:** ~12–16 working sets
- Push Press OR Landmine Push Press: 5 × 3 @ RPE 7 (fast)
- Bench Press: 3 × 3–5 @ RPE 8
- Weighted Pull-Up / Heavy Pulldown: 4 × 4–6 @ RPE 8
- Med Ball Chest Pass: 6 × 2
- Hanging Knee Raise (strict): 3 × 8–12

**Thu — Hinge Power + Lateral/Decel**
- **Focus:** Fast hinge + reactive lateral work
- **Volume:** ~12–16 working sets
- Trap Bar Deadlift (speed): 6 × 2 @ ~60–70% e1RM (RPE 6–7, bar speed priority)
- Lateral Bound to Stick → (if tolerated) Lateral Bound Continuous: 4–6 × 3/side — *progress to reactive control carefully*
- Single-Leg RDL: 3 × 6/side @ RPE 7–8
- Step-Down or Lateral Step-Down: 3 × 6/side @ controlled eccentric — *decel & knee alignment*
- Copenhagen Plank: 2–3 × 20–30s/side

**Sat — Repeat Power / Capacity Circuit (Ski-Style)**
- **Focus:** Repeated bursts + trunk stiffness under fatigue
- **Volume:** ~10–16 working sets
- Sled Push (if available) OR Bike Sprint: 8–12 × 10–15s hard / 60–90s easy — *repeat power without high landing stress*
- Leg Press (moderate): 3 × 8 @ RPE 7–8
- Cable Woodchop (controlled): 3 × 10/side
- Suitcase Carry: 4 × 20–30m/side

## Progression Strategy (Phase 3)
- Power work progresses by **quality first**:
  - Add a set only if jump height/speed stays consistent and landings are stable.
- Strength maintenance stays at **RPE 7–8** with modest volume.
- **Deload every 4th week**: halve contacts/sets, keep movement speed sharp.
- Transition when: you can express speed (no grinding), and knee tolerance remains good with reactive progressions.

---

# Phase 4: Pre-Season Specificity → In-Season Maintenance (Weeks 23+; cycle into season)
Shift toward ski-like repeatability, eccentric control, and maintenance of strength/power with lower fatigue cost.

## Weekly Pattern (typical)
**Mon — Power + Strength Microdose (Lower)**
- Jumps (low contacts): 4 × 2
- Squat pattern: 3 × 2–3 @ RPE 7–8
- Single-leg strength (split squat/step-up): 2–3 × 5–6/side @ RPE 7–8
- Core anti-rotation: 2–3 sets

**Tue — Upper Maintenance**
- Bench or overhead press: 3 × 3–5 @ RPE 7–8
- Row/pull: 3–4 × 5–8 @ RPE 7–8
- Trunk: 2–3 sets

**Thu — Decel + Posterior Chain (Knee Friendly)**
- Hinge (trap bar/RDL): 3 × 3–5 @ RPE 7–8
- Step-down / lateral step-down: 2–3 × 6/side
- Copenhagen + calves: 2–3 sets each

**Sat — Capacity / Repeat Power (low joint cost)**
- Sled/bike intervals: 10–20 min
- Optional light full-body circuit (2–3 rounds)

## Progression Strategy (Phase 4)
- Goal is **maintenance and freshness**: keep intensity moderate-high, keep volume low.
- Deload as needed around increased on-snow volume, travel, or soreness trends (often every 3–5 weeks in-season).

---

## Phase Cycling
**Foundation (6 wks) → Max Strength (8 wks) → Power/RFD (8 wks) → Pre-Season/Season Maintenance (ongoing)**

**Triggers to move phases**
- Time-based progression is the default, but you’ll hold or regress plyos if:
  - Knee pain increases >2/10 during or next day
  - Swelling/instability sensations occur
  - Landing mechanics degrade (valgus collapse, loud landings)
- If strength numbers stall with good recovery: add a small volume bump (1 set on primary lift) or extend Phase 2 by 2 weeks before moving to Phase 3.

**Baseline testing (recommended in Week 1 and again at Phase transitions)**
- e1RM estimates: front/SSB squat, trap bar deadlift, RFESS 6RM each side
- Single-leg quality: step-down control, single-leg squat to box, hop-to-stick (pain-free)
- Power: CMJ height (or best-of-3 jump consistency), broad jump optional

---

## Modification History
- **2026-02-27:** Initial program created from intake profile. Emphasized unilateral strength, controlled lateral plyos, adductor/hip stability (Copenhagens/hip airplanes), and RPE-based progression with 4-week deload rhythm to respect prior right MCL sprain and off-season power goals.
